frameworks.name: subjective inflation
frameworks.one_line: unattended peripheral stimuli appear stronger than attended ones at equated performance

frameworks.name: theories rejecting sensory sufficiency for consciousness
frameworks.one_line: frameworks claiming conscious perception requires more than sensory signals

frameworks.name: psychometric function analysis
frameworks.one_line: new analytic approach quantifying inflation across full functions, not single matched points

core_question: Does subjective inflation under inattention robustly occur, and under what conditions?
key_assumptions: visibility and performance thresholds can be dissociated
attention modulates visibility and performance thresholds independently
key_findings: robust inattentional inflation for contrast-dependent and texture-based perception at and above threshold
at suprathreshold, inflation occurs for overall stimulus but not the performance-relevant feature
inflation occurs if and only if attention reduces performance thresholds more than visibility thresholds
interpretations: subjective experience can systematically exceed visual discrimination capacity
results constrain theories of conscious perception by specifying when inflation arises
